title_es: Batalla de Palomitas: Aire vs. Microondas
hook_en: What's really in your popcorn? Let's find out!
hook_es: ¿Qué hay realmente en tus palomitas? ¡Vamos a descubrirlo!
key_message: Microwave popcorn may contain harmful chemicals, while air-popped is a safer, healthier choice.
visual_elements: Split screen with a bowl of air-popped popcorn on one side and a bag of microwave popcorn on the other
Close-up shot of the popcorn popping process for each method
Text overlay highlighting 'PFAS chemicals' with an arrow pointing to the microwave popcorn bag
Final scene with the creator holding a bowl of air-popped popcorn, smiling
_source_food_context: • HEALTHY: Air-popped popcorn with real butter vs. PROCESSED: Microwave popcorn (diacetyl, PFAS chemicals in bag lining) — Microwave popcorn bags are lined with PFAS (forever chemicals) that leach into food

title_en: DIY Popcorn: Taste Without the Toxins
hook_en: Want better popcorn? Ditch the microwave!
key_message: Making popcorn at home with real butter is a natural, toxin-free alternative to microwave popcorn.
visual_elements: Quick cuts of the creator measuring popcorn kernels and melting real butter
Side-by-side shot of a microwave popcorn bag and a bowl of freshly air-popped popcorn
Animated text listing 'Diacetyl' and other chemicals with a red cross over them
Final shot of the creator eating air-popped popcorn with a thumbs-up
_source_food_context: • HEALTHY: Air-popped popcorn with real butter vs. PROCESSED: Microwave popcorn (diacetyl, PFAS chemicals in bag lining) — Microwave popcorn bags are lined with PFAS (forever chemicals) that leach into food
